body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:live-session

## Reasoning

This is a REASSESSMENT of a previously non-green cell (prior verdict: yes / bug / ready, recorded known_failing 3/4). Re-derived from scratch against opencode CLI v1.14.50 and the current daemon code. The prior `daemon_capability: bug` was a MISLABEL: the daemon does not mis-handle the terminal settle â€” it is a process-exit-before-poll RACE that is owned by the recording driver, not the daemon. The verdict flips to daemon_capability: full, and the real non-green axis is the driver.

long-agentic-session-stress asserts the daemon keeps a long, tool-heavy session coherently observed: state stays continuously `working` across MANY interleaved assistant-message / tool-call / tool-result iterations, no spurious flicker to ready/waiting mid-work, and a clean settle to ready when the turn ends. opencode supports tool calls (capabilities.json tool_calls:true) and in headless default (`opencode run`) auto-executes them inside its own sandbox with no permission gate, so the burst never enters waiting. Session state lives in opencode's SQLite store; the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source polls the UUID-keyed ses_â€¦ session.

Daemon path (full): the opencode watcher (watcher.go scanParts) reads new part rows and emits one EventActivity per scan, setting Terminal=true when it sees a step-finish part with a turn-ending reason (isTerminalPart: stop/interrupted/length/error/content-filter) or a message-level error (isErrorMessage, #493). A tool-calls step-finish reason is NOT terminal, so a long writeâ†’readâ†’edit tool sequence inside one turn stays one contiguous `working` span â€” exactly the coherence this scenario stresses. The settle to ready happens in the application layer: session_detector_activity.go short-circuits the debounce on a Terminal activity and calls processActivity, which records the workingâ†’ready transition ("agent finished turn â†’ ready"). metrics.go carries no state logic (pure token accounting). This settle path is PROVEN correct on the sibling headless recordings basic-turn (events.jsonl seq 701 workingâ†’ready lands 39ms BEFORE process_exited at seq 702) and self-correction-iteration (ready seq 4203 lands 12ms before process_exited seq 4204) â€” same driver, same source, clean settle. So given the terminal step-finish part while the process is alive, the daemon settles correctly: daemon_capability is full.

Driver gap (root cause of the prior known_failing): the existing recording's events.jsonl shows the long turn's terminal step-finish reason=stop landing in the WAL at seq 4761 (07.379s) â€” ~175ms AFTER process_exited at seq 4760 (07.204s). Because headless `opencode run` exits at turn end, the daemon's poller never reads the terminal part while the session's process is alive, so no terminal workingâ†’ready transition is recorded (no "agent finished turn â†’ ready" line; the session is left in `working` at observation end, with only debounce_terminal at seq 4762). This is a timing race created by the DRIVER killing the process at turn end, not the daemon mis-parsing anything â€” and it is exactly how OPEN issue #492 frames it (labeled enhancement / scope:tooling, NOT bug): "the headless `opencode run` process exits at turn end, so the final step-finish reason=stop lands in the SQLite store ~140â€“175ms after process_exited, and the terminal workingâ†’ready settle is missed. A long-lived REPL process would fix that too."

The fix mechanism already exists in the driver: #495 added a LIVE-TUI path (run_live) to drive-opencode-interactive.sh that launches the opencode TUI under tmux and keeps ONE process alive across the whole step-script (send / wait_turn / reset_session / slash / sleep). A long-lived process never exits at turn end, so the daemon polls the terminal step-finish while the session is still alive and the workingâ†’ready settle lands normally â€” clearing the race. BUT the routing only engages run_live when the recipe carries a `reset_session` or `slash` step (drive-opencode-interactive.sh line ~355); this scenario's recipe is send/wait_turn/sleep only, so it still routes to the headless path and hits the race. Hence driver_capability: gap:live-session â€” the live/long-lived-process step path exists but this recipe is not wired to it. This is queued tooling work (route this scenario to run_live, or add an opt-in), NOT a daemon bug and NOT a freeze.

MODEL note: opencode's default is the local LM Studio model lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored (~/.config/opencode/opencode.json); the driver passes no --model. gemma 2B is slow and may be unreliable at sustaining a long multi-tool loop; if it will not sustain the burst that is a model/infra limit, not a daemon/opencode incapability (tool_calls:true).
caveats: Reconciled post-record (2026-05-27): routed onto the long-lived run_live path via the 'live' marker (commit f6919c45); cell recorded 4/4 green â€” driver_capability gap:live-session->ready.

The recordable target is now via the live-TUI driver path (run_live, added in #495), which keeps one opencode TUI process alive across the whole turn so the terminal step-finish is polled before exit. The current recipe (send/wait_turn/sleep) routes to the headless path; route it to run_live (the driver opts in on reset_session/slash steps today) to clear the race and re-record toward green.
opencode exposes no CLI-assignable session IDs; the driver discovers the ses_â€¦ session id by querying SQLite keyed on the per-run cwd.

The phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the stress is asserted via the single sustained working span + the no-waiting invariant; the high tool-call volume (â‰Ą5 function_call/output pairs) is verified in the SQLite store / transcript out-of-band at record time.

The local gemma 2B default model is slow and may be unreliable at sustaining a LONG multi-tool loop in one turn; if it will not sustain the burst the limitation is the model, not opencode/daemon (capabilities.json tool_calls:true).

sources.note: PROVES the miss is a process-exit race, not daemon mishandling: terminal step-finish lands as transcript_activity at seq 4761 (07.379s) ~175ms AFTER process_exited at seq 4760 (07.204s); no terminal state_transitionâ†’ready is recorded â€” the session is left in working with only debounce_terminal (seq 4762).

PROVES the daemon settle path is correct (daemon_capability: full): same headless driver, workingâ†’ready ("agent finished turn â†’ ready") at seq 701 lands 39ms BEFORE process_exited at seq 702 â€” when the terminal step-finish is polled while the process is alive, the daemon settles cleanly. self-correction-iteration shows the same (seq 4203 ready 12ms before seq 4204 process_exited).

scanParts/isTerminalPart: a step-finish with reason stop/interrupted/length/error/content-filter sets Terminal=true on EventActivity; a tool-calls reason is non-terminal so a long multi-tool turn stays ONE working span. metrics.go carries no state logic â€” settle is entirely activity-driven.

On a Terminal activity the detector short-circuits the debounce and calls processActivity, which records the workingâ†’ready settle. Process exit (KindProcessExited, pid_manager.go) emits no state transition, so a terminal part arriving AFTER process_exited produces no settle â€” the driver-owned race.

OPEN, labeled enhancement / scope:tooling / scope:opencode â€” frames this cell's known_failing explicitly as a DRIVER problem ('headless `opencode run` process exits at turn end â€¦ the terminal workingâ†’ready settle is missed. A long-lived REPL process would fix that too.'). Not a daemon bug.

run_live (added in #495) launches the opencode TUI under tmux and keeps ONE process alive across send/wait_turn/reset_session/slash/sleep â€” the long-lived path that clears the #492 race. Routing (line ~355) engages it only on reset_session/slash steps; this recipe (send/wait_turn/sleep) still routes headless, hence driver_capability: gap:live-session.
